meaning: vt.征税,强加,以...欺骗vi.利用,欺骗,施影响
examples: He has tried to impose solutions to the country's problems by fiat.
She didn't want to impose her values on her family.
I didn't want to impose myself on my married friends.
In a British media report Thursday, Siegel warned the group would probably have to impose compulsory layoffs.
Anti-money-laundering laws do impose high costs, and that cost is borne by the financial industry and its consumers.
